metadata.source: Historical archives, Malaysian Government Gazettes, corporate annual reports, and economic development plans (Dasar Ekonomi Baru to MyDIGITAL)
metadata.description: Comprehensive dataset of Malaysian actors driving technical change. Batch 1 of 5.
metadata.introduction: A granular view of the dynamics structuring technical change in Malaysia. This batch covers foundational state apparatus, key research institutes, major universities, and strategic industrial conglomerates. Events detail the evolution from an agriculture-based economy to an industrialized nation and a digital economy hub.

actors.name: Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (MOSTI)
actors.description: Principal government body for science, technology, and innovation policy.

events.description: Established as Ministry of Technology, Research and Local Government.
Redesignated as Ministry of Science, Technology and Environment (MOSTE).
Renamed Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (MOSTI).
Split scope; redefined focus on science and technology research.

actors.name: Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA)
actors.description: Principal agency for promoting investment in manufacturing and services.

events.description: Established as Federal Industrial Development Authority (FIDA).
Rebranded as Malaysian Investment Development Authority to cover services.

actors.name: PETRONAS (Petroliam Nasional Berhad)
actors.description: National oil company driving downstream industrialization and R&D.

events.description: Incorporated under the Petroleum Development Act.
Commissioned the first polypropylene plant, entering petrochemicals.
Launched the PETRONAS Twin Towers, symbolizing technological ambition.
Established PETRONAS Digital to accelerate digital transformation.

actors.name: SIRIM Berhad
actors.description: National standards and industrial research institute.

events.description: Established as Standards and Industrial Research Institute of Malaysia (SIRIM).
Corporatised as SIRIM Berhad under Ministry of Finance.
Launched the Auto-Cooker project for small-medium enterprises.

actors.name: MIMOS Berhad
actors.description: National R&D centre for semiconductors, microelectronics, and ICT.

events.description: Established as Malaysian Institute of Microelectronic Systems.
Launched the 'Wi-Fi Mesh' technology for rural connectivity.

actors.name: Universiti Malaya (UM)
actors.description: Malaysia's oldest university, a key producer of technical elites.

events.description: Founded as King Edward VII College of Medicine in Singapore.
Established as Universiti Malaya in Kuala Lumpur.
Ranked among world's top 100 for Engineering & Technology.

actors.name: Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM)
actors.description: Premier public technical university focusing on engineering and technology.

events.description: Origins traced to Technical School in Kuala Lumpur.
Relocated main campus to Skudai, Johor, creating a massive tech hub.

actors.name: Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB)
actors.description: State-owned utility responsible for power generation and transmission.

actors.name: Ministry of International Trade and Industry (MITI)
actors.description: Leads Malaysia's industrialization strategy and trade policies.

events.description: Established as Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Renamed Ministry of Trade and Industry.
Launched the Second Industrial Master Plan (IMP2).

actors.name: Malaysian Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC)
actors.description: Lead agency driving digital adoption and tech startup ecosystem.

events.description: Established as Multimedia Development Corporation (MDeC) to manage MSC Malaysia.
Rebranded as Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation.
Launched MyDIGITAL initiative to transform Malaysia into a digitally-driven economy.

actors.name: Proton Holdings Berhad
actors.description: Malaysia's first national car manufacturer, symbol of industrialization.

events.description: Launched the Proton Saga, the first Malaysian car.
DRB-HICOM signed deal with Geely for strategic partnership and tech transfer.

actors.name: Sime Darby Plantation
actors.description: World's largest palm oil producer, driving agricultural mechanization.

events.description: Merger of Sime Darby, Golden Hope, and Kumpulan Guthrie.
Implemented drone technology for plantation mapping and aerial spraying.

actors.name: Top Glove Corporation Berhad
actors.description: World's largest manufacturer of gloves, critical for medical technology.

events.description: Expanded into surgical gloves production with higher technical specs.

actors.name: Telekom Malaysia Berhad (TM)
actors.description: National telecommunications company driving broadband connectivity.

actors.name: Cyberjaya
actors.description: Malaysia's first cyber city and tech pioneer hub.

events.description: Launched by Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamad.
Opened the Cyberview Lodge and Data Center cluster.

actors.name: Malaysian Rubber Board (MRB)
actors.description: R&D agency for the rubber industry, focusing on biotechnology and engineering.

events.description: Established as Rubber Research Institute of Malaya.

actors.name: Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB)
actors.description: R&D agency driving palm oil downstream processing and biofuel technology.

events.description: Established as Palm Oil Research Institute of Malaysia (PORIM).
Merged with Palm Oil Registration and Licensing Authority (PORLA) to form MPOB.

actors.name: Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (MARDI)
actors.description: Leading agency for food and agricultural technology research.

actors.name: Forest Research Institute Malaysia (FRIM)
actors.description: Leading institute for tropical forestry research and timber technology.

actors.name: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM)
actors.description: Central bank overseeing financial stability and FinTech innovation.

events.description: Central Bank of Malaya Ordinance enacted.
Issued Financial Technology Regulatory Sandbox Framework.

actors.name: Securities Commission Malaysia (SC)
actors.description: Regulates the capital market and equity crowdfunding platforms.

events.description: Established under the Securities Commission Act 1993.

actors.name: Bursa Malaysia Berhad
actors.description: Stock exchange operator facilitating capital for technology firms.

events.description: Demutualised and listed on its own exchange.
Launched 'Leading Entrepreneur Accelerator Platform (LEAP) Market' for SMEs.

actors.name: Intel Malaysia
actors.description: Major semiconductor assembly and test facility, anchor of the E&E sector.

events.description: Established first offshore assembly plant in Penang.
Opened design center for chip architecture.

actors.name: Inari Amertron Berhad
actors.description: Leading Malaysian contract electronics manufacturer.

actors.name: Western Digital (Malaysia)
actors.description: Major manufacturer of hard drives, key component of local tech ecosystem.

actors.name: Infineon Technologies (Malaysia)
actors.description: Key facility for automotive and industrial electronics.

actors.name: Perodua (Perusahaan Otomobil Kedua)
actors.description: Second national car manufacturer, strong in engine manufacturing.

events.description: Opened new manufacturing plant in Rawang.

actors.name: Khazanah Nasional Berhad
actors.description: Sovereign wealth fund investing in strategic high-tech sectors.

events.description: Launched Khazanah Impact Programme for social tech.

actors.name: Employees Provident Fund (EPF)
actors.description: Major investor in technology infrastructure and startups.

actors.name: Penang Skills Development Centre (PSDC)
actors.description: First tri-partite skills training centre in Malaysia (Gov, Industry, Academia).

events.description: Established to support the electronics cluster.

actors.name: Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM)
actors.description: National university with strong focus on environmental and biotech research.

actors.name: Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM)
actors.description: Premier research university, leader in sustainability and microelectronics.

events.description: Established as second university in Malaysia.
Conferred APEX University status for transformation.

actors.name: Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM)
actors.description: Leading university for agricultural and forestry technology.

events.description: Renamed Universiti Putra Malaysia to reflect broader scope.

actors.name: Universiti Teknologi PETRONAS (UTP)
actors.description: Specialized engineering university funded by PETRONAS.

events.description: Moved to iconic campus in Bandar Seri Iskandar.

actors.name: Multimedia University (MMU)
actors.description: First private university focused on ICT and multimedia.

actors.name: Taylor's University
actors.description: Leading private university in hospitality and engineering technology.

actors.name: Sunway University
actors.description: Private university with strong links to Jeffrey Cheah Foundation and tech research.

actors.name: Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC)
actors.description: Regulatory body for communications and multimedia.

events.description: Established under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.
Implemented Mandatory Standards on Access Pricing (MSAP).

actors.name: Maxis Berhad
actors.description: Leading integrated communications provider.

actors.name: CelcomDigi Berhad
actors.description: Largest mobile operator by subscriber base, formed by merger.

events.description: Celcom established as first mobile operator.
Completed merger of Celcom and Digi.

actors.name: Grab Malaysia
actors.description: Southeast Asian tech giant originating from Malaysia, driving digital payments.

events.description: Launched GrabPay and fintech services regionally.

actors.name: AirAsia Group
actors.description: Pioneer of low-cost travel, pivoting to digital travel tech.

events.description: Acquired by Tony Fernandes, revolutionized low-cost model.

actors.name: Touch 'n Go Group
actors.description: Key player in digital payments and transit solutions.

events.description: Launched smart card for toll payment.

actors.name: Maybank
actors.description: Largest bank in Malaysia, leading in digital banking transformation.

events.description: Launched MAE (Maybank Anywhere Everyone) virtual banking.

actors.name: CIMB Group
actors.description: Major ASEAN bank driving digital trade finance.

events.description: Acquired Southern Bank, expanding tech capabilities.

actors.name: Malaysia Airlines Berhad
actors.description: National carrier, hub for aviation engineering and maintenance.

actors.description: Promotes the development of the aerospace manufacturing ecosystem.

actors.description: Key manufacturer of aerostructures.

actors.name: DRB-HICOM Berhad
actors.description: Major player in automotive, banking, and infrastructure.

events.description: Formed from the merger of Diversified Resources and Heavy Industries Corp.

actors.name: Axiata Group Berhad
actors.description: Telecoms conglomerate with operations across ASEAN and South Asia.

actors.name: Time dotCom Berhad
actors.description: Provider of fixed line and data services, focusing on fiber.

actors.name: Globetronics Technology Berhad
actors.description: Contract manufacturer for semiconductor and optoelectronics.

actors.description: Provider of semiconductor assembly and test services.

actors.description: Global leader in nitrile glove manufacturing technology.

actors.name: Kossan Rubber Industries Berhad
actors.description: Technical rubber and glove manufacturer.

actors.name: Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) - MLNG
actors.description: Operator of LNG complexes, critical for gas processing tech.

actors.name: Sarawak Energy Berhad

events.description: Established as Sarawak Electricity Supply Company.

actors.name: Sapura Energy Berhad
actors.description: Global integrated oil and gas services provider.

actors.name: Dialog Group Berhad
actors.description: Technical services provider to the oil and gas industry.

actors.name: Ibraco Berhad
actors.description: Construction and property developer with technical civil engineering focus.

actors.name: German-Malaysian Institute (GMI)

events.description: Established as a joint project between Malaysia and Germany.

actors.name: Japan-Malaysia Technical Institute (JMTI)

actors.name: Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE)

actors.name: Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA)

actors.description: Promotes green technology adoption.

actors.name: MRB Rubber Research Institute of Malaysia (RRIM)
